chunks.page_summary: This page introduces the Rheonics SR-STCM, a Smart Thermal Control Module for SRV and SRD viscometers and density sensors, highlighting its compact design, precise temperature control capabilities, and various connectivity options.
chunks.extracted_text:
rheonics
SR-STCM
SRV and SRD Smart Thermal Control Module

*   5 °C to 120 °C
*   0.005 °C temp. stability
*   0.05 °C temp. accuracy
*   Integrated temperature controller
*   Standalone operation
*   Ethernet, Wi-Fi, RS485, USB ports for direct integration
*   Desktop and in-line units

STCM is custom designed to ensure uniform temperature distribution across SRV and/or SRD even when installed in a flow loop with moving fluids. Heating and cooling is achieved through solid-state thermal devices with an advanced model-based controller. STCM was designed after extensive simulation and testing to ensure high level of thermal stability, accuracy and repeatability.

STCM eliminates the need for bulky and expensive thermal test chambers (climate chambers) for temperature control. It reduces the foot print to the same size as a typical small coffee machine while achieving 10x better stability and uniformity compared to a 100L commercial thermal chamber. It enables fluid analysis with SRV in field and mobile units without compromising data integrity.

SRV Accessories - STCM
©Rheonics
2310
key_facts: The SR-STCM is a Smart Thermal Control Module designed for SRV and SRD sensors.
It ensures uniform temperature distribution across SRV and/or SRD, even when installed in a flow loop with moving fluids.
Heating and cooling are achieved through solid-state thermal devices with an advanced model-based controller.
The STCM was designed after extensive simulation and testing to ensure high thermal stability, accuracy, and repeatability.
The STCM eliminates the need for bulky and expensive thermal test chambers for temperature control.
Its footprint is reduced to the same size as a typical small coffee machine.
It achieves 10x better stability and uniformity compared to a 100L commercial thermal chamber.
The module enables fluid analysis with SRV in field and mobile units without compromising data integrity.
It features an integrated temperature controller and supports standalone operation.
Connectivity options include Ethernet, Wi-Fi, RS485, and USB ports for direct integration.
The unit is suitable for both desktop and in-line applications.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides detailed specifications for the Rheonics STCM inline viscometer and density sensor, including its physical dimensions, power requirements, operational temperature ranges, communication interfaces, and a thermal uniformity visualization of an SRV sensor.
chunks.extracted_text:
rheonics
Thermal uniformity across the SRV during operation along with the fluid chamber

STCM Specification

Parameter | Value | Notes
---|---|---
Dimensions | 314 x 165 x 450 (mm) | As big as a typical desktop coffee machine
 | 12.4 x 6.5 x 17.72 (inch) |
Power Input | 110 / 220 V AC | Mains supply
Power consumption | max. 650W |
Total Weight | 11.6 kg (25.6 lb) | Including SRV/SRD and fluid cell
Temperature range | 5 °C to 120 °C |
 | 40 °F to 250 °F |
Sample Fluid | Sample volume 22ml | Both static and inline flow-through models available
 | Inline flow through model (STCM-FT) |
Temperature stability | 0.005 °C |
Temperature accuracy | 0.05 °C | Depends on calibration required
Ambient temperature | max. 65 °C |
 | max. 150 °F |
Controller | Integrated SMEBOX | Standalone Controller and Integrated CPU
Communication | Wi-Fi, USB-C, RS-485 (Modbus), Ethernet (TCP/IP, Modbus TCP) | Direct control of STCM and SRV/SRD from RCP software
Communication (Sensor direct) | Ethernet, RS-485 (Modbus), USB, SD-Card, Bluetooth LTE4.0, 4-20 mA | Same as a standard SRV/SRD electronics. Accessible through the integrated SMEBOX.

SRV Accessories - STCM
©Rheonics
2310

specifications.Communication: Wi-Fi, USB-C, RS-485 (Modbus), Ethernet (TCP/IP, Modbus TCP)
specifications.Communication (Sensor direct): Ethernet, RS-485 (Modbus), USB, SD-Card, Bluetooth LTE4.0, 4-20 mA

key_facts: The STCM has dimensions comparable to a typical desktop coffee machine.
The power input for the STCM is 110 / 220 V AC, supplied from mains.
The total weight of 11.6 kg (25.6 lb) includes the SRV/SRD sensor and fluid cell.
Both static and inline flow-through models are available for sample fluid handling.
The STCM controller is an Integrated SMEBOX, which functions as a standalone controller with an integrated CPU.
The communication interfaces allow for direct control of the STCM and SRV/SRD from RCP software.
Direct sensor communication options are equivalent to a standard SRV/SRD electronics.
warnings_notes: Temperature accuracy of 0.05 °C depends on the calibration required.
Direct sensor communication is accessible through the integrated SMEBOX.

chunks.page_summary: This page details the features of Rheonics RCP Software for data acquisition and temperature control, highlighting its application with the STCM accessory for comprehensive fluid property measurements and analysis.
chunks.extracted_text:
rheonics

[Rheonics Control Panel 5.4.0.4 Software Interface]

Key Features:
Simultaneous fluid measurement & precision temperature control
Constant temperature mode
Temperature ramps with varying dwell times
Target temperature modes – constant and ramp
Data export to excel and other statistical software

STCM Description:
STCM is built for calibration verification, viscosity and density measurements at various temperatures, estimating temperature compensation factors for viscosity and density, estimation of activation energy from Arrhenius viscosity models.

Footer:
SRV Accessories - STCM
©Rheonics
2310
key_facts: The RCP Software provides capabilities for data acquisition and temperature control.
It enables simultaneous fluid measurement alongside precision temperature control.
The software supports both constant temperature mode and temperature ramps with user-configurable dwell times.
Target temperature modes can be set as either constant or ramp profiles.
Data acquired through the software can be exported for analysis in Excel and other statistical software.
The STCM accessory is designed for calibrating and verifying viscosity and density measurements across a range of temperatures.
STCM aids in determining temperature compensation factors for both viscosity and density.
The system can be used to estimate activation energy based on Arrhenius viscosity models.
The Rheonics Control Panel version displayed is 5.4.0.4.
